ONE cutover-compatibility CONSTRAINT (make the migration safe regardless of identity-cutover state): the identity cutover's M-REPOINT (freeze-only, already applied prod/staging) repoints FKs profiles->person, so concepts.profile_id's FK TARGET differs by env (->profiles on dev, ->person on prod/staging). Keep your RLS predicate COLUMN-BASED —  as you stated — and do NOT join to profiles (or any identity table) inside the USING/WITH CHECK. A column-comparison predicate is FK-target-agnostic => identical behavior in every env, cutover or not. The concept tables themselves are NOT dropped by the cutover (they're concept tables, not identity tables), so no structural conflict. Dispatch the Sonnet Builder; Gate-1 merge stays yours; red-green-revert on the negative-isolation case is the must-have. Report at PR-green or blocker.

ONE VERIFICATION ASK before you call it done: red-green-revert the TIGHTENED test, not just run it — (a) passes with the policy in place, (b) when you temporarily DROP the concept_mastery WITH CHECK policy it fails with the 42501 RLS error specifically (not a generic throw), (c) restore. A test that asserts 42501 but would also pass on an unrelated error is not tight enough; the revert proves it keys on the RLS rejection. Do the same sanity for the USING read-isolation test if not already. Predicate approval STANDS (test-only edit, 0125 SQL unchanged). green -> Gate-1 merge -> /cosmo:execute complete. Report at merge.

Your schema-mismatch LOCATION is right (WI-972/#1477/d0ac76385 tightened the session.completed event schema), but "latent PROD regression / not quarantinable" is OVER-STATED. Field = mode: z.string().optional() (packages/schemas/src/inngest-events.ts:544) → accepts ABSENT, rejects null. BOTH real producers are safe: session-filing-dispatch.ts:109 OMITS via `...(completion.mode ? {mode} : {})` (truthy-guard, never null); session-stale-cleanup.ts sends NO mode. session.completed is server-only (inngest.send) — no client producer. So NO prod path emits mode:null; the optional schema accepts every real event incl. freeform (mode omitted). The ONLY emitter of mode:null is the STALE TEST FIXTURE in session-completed.integration.test.ts (happy-path + freeform) — explicit null instead of omitting like the producer. => stale-test-fixture, NOT a prod regression, and IS test-fixable. FIX (minimal): correct the integration fixtures to OMIT mode (or send a valid string) to match the producer contract; do NOT loosen schema to .nullable() — the producer truthy-omit guard shows optional-not-nullable is INTENTIONAL. OWNERSHIP: you take it now (held-waiting on exactly this + have context). /cosmo:capture a WI — P1 on URGENCY (required main red blocks ALL merges) but body MUST state "test-fixture-stale, not prod regression" so review does not over-scope. Land fixture fix on main → main green → Gate-1 merge WI-1104 → then 1086/323/979. Good STOP+escalate instinct; I only corrected the blast-radius. Report at main-green + 1104 merged.

PRODUCER CONTRACT CONFIRMED (the line you could not grep): session-filing-dispatch.ts:109 = `...(completion.mode ? { mode: completion.mode } : {})` — truthy-guard, so a null/undefined/empty mode is OMITTED, never sent as null. session-stale-cleanup.ts sends NO mode field at all. Both = absent, which z.string().optional() accepts. So OMIT-to-match-producer is exactly right; schema stays unchanged. TWO NUANCES for the Builder: (1) KEEP the valid mode:"relearn" fixture as-is. (2) For the ~10 mode:null fixtures — omit the key, but first eyeball each: if any test EXISTS specifically to assert mode-handling/semantics (not just carrying mode incidentally), omitting may change its intent → adjust that assertion to the real contract (absent→optional accepted) rather than silently dropping coverage.
